<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me first check the center cell structure of bottom-right panel
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Find center cell objects in bottom-right panel

    br = n['boundary']
    r, c = br[0]
    # Center cell is around (44, 44) to (50, 52)
    if 42 <= r <= 52 and 42 <= c <= 54 and n['pixels'] < 36:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, pos=({r},{c}), boundary={br}")

# Let me also check the gray corner decorations
print("\nGray corner decorations:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 20:
        br = n['boundary']
        r, c = br[0]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, pos=({r},{c}), boundary={br}")

# Let me try clicking on the center of the bottom-right center cell (around row 46, col 48)
# But first, let me try resetting to all blue and then try the AND pattern
# Actually, let me try a different approach: enumerate all possible patterns and see which one makes sense

# Maybe the center cell encodes which blocks to toggle from all-blue?
# Let me check: if center cell gray positions correspond to blocks that should be red...

# For top-left panel:
# TL blocks: bRb/R?b/bRR → red at (0,1), (1,0), (2,1), (2,2)
# TL center: g at (0,0), (0,1), (1,0), (1,1) → doesn't match red positions

# Let me try clicking on the gray corner decoration

    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 34, 'col': 34},  # Top-left gray corner of bottom-right panel
]
result = action(actions)
print("\nClick on gray corner result:", last_action_result)
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed', False))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
